title: Aureline work-item offline-handoff packet vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the offline_handoff_packet_record frozen in docs/work_items/work_item_contract.md. One record family preserves a work-item update that the user authored when the provider was unreachable, the system browser was blocked, the workspace was restricted, the freshness floor was unsatisfied, the policy bundle had rolled, the account mapping was pending, the step-up authenticator was pending, or the user explicitly deferred the action into a review packet. The packet pins the bound work_item_detail_record, the snapshotted current_state_rows / owner_or_assignee_rows / engineering_artifact_relations the user saw at capture time, the typed handoff_admission_reason_class, the typed handoff_provider_acceptance_class that explicitly says whether the provider accepted (the safe default is not_submitted_local_capture_only), the typed retry_route_class set the apply path will reopen on, the typed export_route_class set the user can hand the packet through (support-bundle attachment, incident-workspace attachment, companion browser handoff, clipboard / text export, CLI export, managed-admin external handoff, or local-only with no export path), the redaction posture, the optional bound publish-later queue item, the optional bound status-transition packet the offline capture was authored against, and the optional bound provider-callback envelopes that refreshed the packet. The packet never claims the provider has already accepted the update; the publish-later drain runs through a fresh approval ticket on connectivity / browser / account / freshness / policy / trust / step-up resolution. Composes with the work_item_detail_record schema (the bound detail row), the status_transition_packet_record schema (the captured transition the offline packet preserves), the publish-later contract (publish_later_queue_item_record, provider_consequence_preview_record, account_mapping_binding_record, provider_callback_envelope_record, browser_handoff_packet_record), the support / incident contracts (support_bundle_record_id_ref, incident_workspace_packet_record_id_ref, recovery-rung refs), and the issue-routing matrix (handoff_export_route_class composes with the route lane vocabulary). Raw provider URLs, raw provider issue bodies, raw comment bodies, raw label values that disclose customer / tenant identity, raw delegated tokens, raw branch / commit URLs, raw author identity strings, raw absolute paths, and raw notification payloads never cross this boundary regardless of redaction class. The eventual work-items crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new record_kind, handoff_admission_reason_class, handoff_provider_acceptance_class, handoff_export_route_class, handoff_retry_route_class, handoff_drain_state_class, denial_reason, or audit_event_id is additive-minor and bumps offline_handoff_packet_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

offline_handoff_packet_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the offline-handoff packet vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / queue-review rollups / admin-reconciliation consoles. Raw provider URLs, raw provider issue bodies, raw comment bodies, raw delegated tokens, raw branch / commit URLs, raw author identity strings, raw absolute paths, and raw notification payloads never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_label.description: Redaction-aware reviewable label (<= 1024 graphemes). Carries a presentation-only summary safe for support / audit / queue-review / admin-reconciliation surfaces; never carries the raw payload.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show in preview, queue-review, and export review surfaces. Never carries the raw payload.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state the offline-handoff packet was captured under.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class re-exported from ADR-0010 / ADR-0007. Raw payloads never cross this boundary regardless of class.

handoff_admission_reason_class.description: Frozen admission-reason class. Every offline_handoff_packet_record names exactly one. The two imported_from_* values mark the packet as evidence-only with no live provider path; every other value pins a typed prerequisite the apply path watches.

handoff_provider_acceptance_class.description: Frozen provider-acceptance class. **The default for an offline-captured packet is not_submitted_local_capture_only.** The packet MUST NOT silently advance to provider_accept_confirmed_publish_later_drained; the publish-later drain runs through a fresh approval ticket and a fresh provider-callback envelope, and the offline-handoff packet is updated by reference to the bound publish_later_queue_item_record and provider_callback_envelope_record only after the provider has accepted. provider_accept_rejected_with_typed_reason MUST carry a non-empty acceptance_rejection_reason.

handoff_export_route_class.description: Frozen export-route class set the user can hand the packet through. local_only_no_export_path is admissible; the packet still preserves the captured update truthfully and the apply path retries on prerequisite resolution. The packet MAY name multiple export routes (e.g. both support_bundle_attachment_by_reference and clipboard_or_text_export_user_initiated).

handoff_retry_route_class.description: Frozen retry-route class set the apply path will reopen on. The packet MAY name multiple retry routes for compound prerequisites; no_retry_imported_evidence_only is admissible only when handoff_admission_reason_class is one of the imported_from_* values.

handoff_drain_state_class.description: Frozen drain-state class. The packet's lifecycle is rendered explicitly on offline-handoff inbox / queue-review / support-export / admin-reconciliation surfaces.

state_family_class.description: Re-exported state-family axis from schemas/work_items/work_item_detail.schema.json. The offline packet snapshots the same axis vocabulary at capture time.

snapshot_state_row.description: One snapshotted state row preserved from the bound work_item_detail_record at capture time. The drain path revalidates against the current detail row; if the upstream state has drifted past the snapshot, the drain denies and the packet flips to drained_publish_later_rejected_with_typed_reason or revoked_by_user_before_drain depending on the user's choice.

snapshot_owner_row.description: One snapshotted owner / assignee / reviewer / reporter / watcher row preserved from the bound work_item_detail_record at capture time.

relation_axis_class.description: One of the five engineering-artifact relation axes from schemas/work_items/work_item_detail.schema.json. Every snapshot row pins exactly one axis.

relation_class_value.description: Verbatim class value from the matching axis enum on the work_item_detail_record at capture time.

linked_record_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the artifact the relation pinned at capture time. Empty when the relation axis was a no_* value.

snapshot_engineering_relation.description: One snapshotted engineering-artifact relation preserved from the bound work_item_detail_record at capture time. Lets a downstream support / hosted-review / queue-review / admin-reconciliation surface render the relation row mechanically without re-fetching the detail row.

origin_disclosure.description: Canonical Aureline origin the packet was captured under. Revalidated at drain; a drain against a mismatched origin denies.

policy_context.description: Policy context the packet was captured under. Revalidated at drain; a policy-epoch roll between capture and drain re-evaluates the packet.

denial_reason.description: Closed denial-reason vocabulary. captured_handoff_must_not_advance_to_accepted_without_callback_envelope is the gate that forbids a drain path from flipping the packet to provider_accept_confirmed_publish_later_drained without citing a non-null linked_provider_callback_envelope_record_id_ref.

audit_event_id.description: Closed audit-event vocabulary for offline-handoff packet lifecycle.

work_item_detail_record_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the bound work_item_detail_record the packet preserves an offline update over.

linked_status_transition_packet_record_i.description: Opaque ref to the bound status_transition_packet_record the offline capture was authored against. Empty only when the offline capture was authored without a structured transition packet (rare; admissible for a comment-only capture).

handoff_export_route_classes.description: Non-empty set of typed export routes the user can hand the packet through. local_only_no_export_path is admissible alone.

handoff_retry_route_classes.description: Non-empty set of typed retry routes the apply path will reopen on. no_retry_imported_evidence_only is admissible alone only on imported_from_* admission reasons.

snapshot_state_rows.description: Snapshotted state rows preserved from the bound work_item_detail_record at capture time.

snapshot_owner_or_assignee_rows.description: Snapshotted owner / assignee / reviewer / reporter / watcher rows. Empty array is admissible (e.g. a brand-new local draft captured offline).

snapshot_engineering_relations.description: Snapshotted engineering-artifact relations. The packet MUST carry one row per axis (issue-to-branch, linked-review, change-intent, validation-evidence, publish-preview) so a downstream consumer never has to re-fetch the detail row to resolve the relation chip.

linked_publish_later_queue_item_record_i.description: Opaque ref to the bound publish_later_queue_item_record the drain admitted the packet into. Required when handoff_drain_state_class is drain_admitted_to_publish_later_queue, drained_publish_later_completed, or drained_publish_later_rejected_with_typed_reason.

linked_provider_consequence_preview_reco.description: Opaque ref to the bound provider_consequence_preview_record the user confirmed against at capture time. Empty when the offline capture was a save_local_draft_only_no_provider_path or inspect_only_no_mutation transition.

linked_account_mapping_binding_record_id.description: Opaque ref to the bound account_mapping_binding_record. Required when handoff_admission_reason_class is account_mapping_pending_user_selection.

linked_browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the bound browser_handoff_packet_record the apply path will route through after the browser becomes available. Required when handoff_admission_reason_class is browser_handoff_blocked_managed_workstation_no_system_browser or browser_handoff_blocked_user_choice.

linked_provider_callback_envelope_record.description: Opaque refs to provider_callback_envelope_records that confirmed (or rejected) provider acceptance after drain. Required non-empty when handoff_provider_acceptance_class is provider_accept_confirmed_publish_later_drained or provider_accept_rejected_with_typed_reason.

linked_support_bundle_record_id_ref.description: Opaque ref to the bound support_bundle_record when handoff_export_route_classes includes support_bundle_attachment_by_reference.

linked_incident_workspace_packet_record_.description: Opaque ref to the bound incident_workspace_packet_record when handoff_export_route_classes includes incident_workspace_attachment_by_reference.

linked_object_handoff_packet_record_id_r.description: Opaque ref to the bound object_handoff_packet_record when handoff_export_route_classes includes object_handoff_packet_attachment_by_reference.

supersedes_offline_handoff_packet_record.description: Opaque ref to a prior offline_handoff_packet_record this one supersedes (e.g. the user re-captured under a refreshed freshness floor or a re-resolved account mapping).

superseded_by_offline_handoff_packet_rec.description: Opaque ref to the offline_handoff_packet_record that superseded this one when handoff_drain_state_class is superseded_by_handoff_packet.

acceptance_rejection_reason.description: Typed denial-reason token from the publish-later contract (provider_handoff stream) or the work_items denial vocabulary explaining why the provider rejected the drained handoff. Required non-empty when handoff_provider_acceptance_class is provider_accept_rejected_with_typed_reason.

captured_freshness_floor_ref.description: Opaque ref to the freshness floor the packet was captured against. Empty only when handoff_admission_reason_class is workspace_trust_unset_or_restricted_capture_only or one of the imported_from_* values.

expires_at.description: Monotonic timestamp after which the packet must not be drained without re-capture. The drain path denies an expired packet with policy_epoch_expired_re_evaluation_required or captured_state_drift_must_deny_silent_drain.

allOf.description: imported_from_* admission reasons MUST resolve handoff_provider_acceptance_class to imported_handoff_evidence_only_no_provider_path AND handoff_retry_route_classes to a single-element [no_retry_imported_evidence_only] set.
no_retry_imported_evidence_only retry route is admissible only on imported_from_* admission reasons.
provider_accept_confirmed_publish_later_drained MUST cite a non-empty linked_publish_later_queue_item_record_id_ref AND a non-empty linked_provider_callback_envelope_record_id_refs.
provider_accept_rejected_with_typed_reason MUST cite a non-empty acceptance_rejection_reason AND a non-empty linked_provider_callback_envelope_record_id_refs.
Browser-blocked admission reasons MUST cite a non-empty linked_browser_handoff_packet_ref.
account_mapping_pending_user_selection admission MUST cite a non-empty linked_account_mapping_binding_record_id_ref.
drain_admitted_to_publish_later_queue / drained_publish_later_completed / drained_publish_later_rejected_with_typed_reason MUST cite a non-empty linked_publish_later_queue_item_record_id_ref.
support_bundle_attachment_by_reference export route MUST cite a non-empty linked_support_bundle_record_id_ref.
incident_workspace_attachment_by_reference export route MUST cite a non-empty linked_incident_workspace_packet_record_id_ref.
object_handoff_packet_attachment_by_reference export route MUST cite a non-empty linked_object_handoff_packet_record_id_ref.
superseded_by_handoff_packet drain state MUST cite a non-empty superseded_by_offline_handoff_packet_record_id_ref.

offline_handoff_packet_record.description: Frozen offline-handoff packet for one work-item update captured under provider unavailability, browser-blocked, restricted-trust, freshness-floor unsatisfied, policy-epoch pending, account-mapping pending, step-up pending, or explicit user-deferred conditions. The packet preserves the captured state rows, owner / assignee rows, and engineering-artifact relations the user saw at capture time, the typed admission / acceptance / export / retry / drain classes, and the optional bound publish-later queue item, status-transition packet, browser-handoff packet, account-mapping, support-bundle, incident-workspace, and object-handoff packet refs. **The packet never claims the provider has accepted the update**; the safe default for handoff_provider_acceptance_class is not_submitted_local_capture_only and the drain path runs through a fresh approval ticket plus a fresh provider-callback envelope before the packet may flip to provider_accept_confirmed_publish_later_drained.

offline_handoff_packet_audit_event_recor.description: Audit-event row for the offline-handoff packet lifecycle. Denial events MUST cite a typed reason from the closed denial vocabulary.
